title: F2-tests: 4-variant coverage for all 6 banners + install-pipeline ships banner-i18n.cjs
scope: packages/cli/test/hooks/banner-i18n.test.ts + integration shadow on existing fabric-hint/knowledge-hint-broad tests
description: Add a dedicated test file `packages/cli/test/hooks/banner-i18n.test.ts` covering all 4 language variants (`en`, `zh-CN`, `zh-CN-hybrid`, `match-existing`) for each of the 6 banners (5 fabric-hint signals + 1 broad hook). Minimum required coverage per variant: substring contract assertion (numeric tokens, signal CTA names, file paths) + protected-token preservation. Add a smoke test that the install pipeline (install/skills-and-hooks.ts) ships `lib/banner-i18n.cjs` alongside both hook .cjs files into the target workspace's `.fabric/.hooks/` (or wherever the existing install code places them).

files.change: New test file: 24 tests minimum (6 banners × 4 variants); each asserts (a) non-empty render, (b) variant-specific substring (e.g. 'h' or '小时' for time), (c) protected-token presence (e.g. '/fabric-archive' verbatim)

files.target: install hook test suite (find appropriate existing test file via grep)
files.change: Add an integration assertion that after `installArchiveHintHook()` and `installKnowledgeHintBroadHook()` run on a temp workspace, the file `<workspace>/.fabric/.hooks/lib/banner-i18n.cjs` exists (or whatever the canonical install path is)

implementation: Create `packages/cli/test/hooks/banner-i18n.test.ts` with vitest describe blocks per banner key
For each variant × banner, assert: render is non-empty, contains expected substring contract, contains expected protected tokens
Add fuzz test: 100 random (variant, params) combinations never throw
Locate the existing install-pipeline test (grep for `installArchiveHintHook` in test/) and add an assertion that lib/banner-i18n.cjs is copied

If the install pipeline does NOT currently copy lib/*.cjs files, file the discrepancy as a sub-finding for TASK-002 to add the copy logic (DO NOT silently let install ship without the lib — hook will fail at runtime)

reference.pattern: Existing fabric-hint test patterns under packages/cli/test/hooks/ (use the same vitest structure, fixture loading, and substring assertions)
reference.examples: Existing fabric-hint.test.ts substring assertions are the model — copy the assertion style and adapt per variant

criteria: File `packages/cli/test/hooks/banner-i18n.test.ts` exists with at least 24 test cases (6 banners × 4 variants)
Each variant test asserts at least one variant-specific substring (proves translation actually happened, not silent fallback)
Install-pipeline integration test asserts `lib/banner-i18n.cjs` is shipped to target workspace alongside the hook .cjs files
Test command: `pnpm --filter @fenglimg/fabric-cli test -- skills-and-hooks` exits 0 (anti-regression for install)
Behavioral expectation: ANY future change to a banner template now requires updating the test file — translation regressions are caught at PR time

rationale.chosen_approach: Dedicated test file per spec (rather than scattering variant tests across existing hook tests) keeps the i18n contract surface searchable and gives a single failure summary when a translation regression lands
alternatives_considered: Inline variant tests inside existing fabric-hint.test.ts (rejected: bloats existing file, harder to grep for i18n coverage)
Skip zh-CN-hybrid + match-existing tests, only en + zh-CN (rejected: user spec explicitly requires all 4)
decision_factors: i18n regressions are silent — must be caught by tests, not manual QA
Install-pipeline coverage is critical: a missing lib file at runtime would crash the hook silently
rationale.tradeoffs: 24+ test cases is verbose, but each is small (1-3 assertions) and follows a uniform shape — easy to maintain

manual_checks: Run the new test file in isolation, confirm it fails if banner-i18n.cjs is broken
Manually delete lib/banner-i18n.cjs from a freshly-installed workspace, confirm the install integration test catches it
success_metrics: Install integration test passes — lib ships correctly
